2606.12300.title: Natural-Language Temporal Grounding in Hour-Long Videos is a Search Problem: A Benchmark and Empirical Decomposition
2606.12300.paper_summary: Temporal grounding--returning the interval $[t_s, t_e]$ for a natural-language query over a video--is the language interface to long-form video, yet has been studied on short videos; the dynamics of hour-scale natural-language grounding remain underexplored. We take the position that at hour-scale, the binding constraint is search, not recognition: Video-LLMs are bottlenecked not by localizing a nearby event, but--given a natural-language query--by searching for the relevant region of a long video. To test this, we release ExtremeWhenBench, the first open hour-scale grounding benchmark (2,273 queries over 194 videos, mean 75.7 min, max 9 hr) with an open-form query distribution. Every open Video-LLM collapses while a frame-level retrieval baseline outperforms them; a failure taxonomy attributes 85% of failures to search; and a retrieve-then-ground hybrid recovers 6.7x over the monolithic Video-LLM--mirroring retrieve-then-read in open-domain QA.
2606.12300.paper_summary_zh: 時間基準——返回自然語言查詢在視頻上的區間 $[t_s, t_e]$——是長格式視頻的語言介面，但卻在短視頻上進行了研究；小時級自然語言基準的動態仍然未被充分探索。我們認為在小時級別，約束條件是搜索，而不是識別：視頻LLM的瓶頸不在於定位附近事件，而是在於根據自然語言查詢搜索長視頻的相關區域。為了測試這一點，我們發布了ExtremeWhenBench，第一個開放的小時級基準（2,273個查詢，涵蓋194個視頻，平均75.7分鐘，最大9小時），並具有開放式查詢分佈。每個開放的視頻LLM都崩潰，而幀級檢索基線則超越了它們；失敗分類法將85%的失敗歸因於搜索；而檢索再基準的混合方法則比單一的視頻LLM恢復了6.7倍——這與開放域QA中的檢索再閱讀相呼應。
2606.12300.author: Sukmin Seo et.al.
